2606.29908.title: Pondering the Way: Spatial-perceiving World Action Model for Embodied Navigation
2606.29908.paper_summary: Existing world model-based planners for visual navigation typically follow a verification-centric paradigm, decoupling goal intent from trajectory synthesis. This approach suffers from candidate dependence, heavy computational overhead, and inconsistencies between sampled actions and predicted visuals. To address these issues, we propose SWAM (Spatial-perceiving World Action Model), a task-centric joint observation-action generation framework. Given start and goal RGB observations, SWAM performs single-pass inference to simultaneously generate intermediate RGB-D sequences and corresponding action trajectories, promoting goal-consistent trajectory generation and improved spatial feasibility. While SWAM leverages depth pseudo-labels during training to internalize spatial priors, it requires only monocular RGB input at inference time. We further introduce a visual-guided action refinement module and a trajectory-scale regularization loss to enforce fine-grained alignment between motion and visual cues while stabilizing predictions across varying distances. Extensive experiments show that SWAM significantly outperforms state-of-the-art two-stage planners in success rate, trajectory accuracy, and inference efficiency, while demonstrating robust zero-shot generalization to unseen environments.
2606.29908.paper_summary_zh: 現有的基於世界模型的視覺導航規劃器通常遵循以驗證為中心的範式，將目標意圖與軌跡合成解耦。這種方法存在候選依賴、計算開銷大以及採樣行動與預測視覺之間的不一致等問題。為了解決這些問題，我們提出了SWAM（空間感知世界行動模型），這是一個以任務為中心的觀察-行動生成框架。給定起始和目標的RGB觀察，SWAM進行單次推理，同時生成中間的RGB-D序列和相應的行動軌跡，促進目標一致的軌跡生成和改善的空間可行性。雖然SWAM在訓練期間利用深度偽標籤來內化空間先驗，但在推理時僅需單目RGB輸入。我們進一步引入了一個視覺引導的行動精煉模塊和一個軌跡尺度正則化損失，以強化運動與視覺線索之間的細緻對齊，同時在不同距離下穩定預測。大量實驗表明，SWAM在成功率、軌跡準確性和推理效率上顯著超越了最先進的兩階段規劃器，同時在未見環境中展示了強大的零樣本泛化能力。
2606.29908.author: Hong Chen et.al.
2606.29908.authors: Hong Chen, Daqi Liu, Zehan Zhang, Haiguang Wang, Tianhao Lu, Longfei Yan, Haiyang Sun, Fangzhen Li, Hongwei Xie, Bing Wang, Guang Chen, Hangjun Ye, Yihua Tan
